lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: How is the adventuring going, my friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other quests for me? That last one of yours was so much fun, I was hoping you would!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ooooh dear, I might have, but it's all very embarrassing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Embarrassing? How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Weeellll... You know I told you that it was the Duke's birthday?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, that's why I had to go and collect you things to make a cake with wasn't it?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yeeeesssss... Weeeelllll...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: It isn't. I don't even know when his birthday is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: GRRRR! Why did you have me running around finding ingredients for you then?!?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you lied to me to get me to do you a favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough, you're not the first, but why lie? You could have just asked me, I'd have been happy to help.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I needed to test you and see if I could trust you...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I am in a terrible pickle, and I desperately need somebody trustworthy to help me out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need help AGAIN? Well, what seems to be the problem?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, you're obviously trustworthy, I guess I could tell you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please do. The suffering of others always seems to turn out very profitable for me.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well... It's like this...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you ever hear of Franizzard Van Lumbcook?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... Can't say I have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And with a name like that, I'm sure I would remember.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, he was a great chef, and also my ancestor.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: One hundred years ago there was a very important meeting held here in Lumbridge Castle, and he cooked a banquet for the meeting, which was so stupendously amazing that he was offered a job for life, along with all

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: of his descendants.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you have your job because a hundred years ago your ancestor was a very good cook.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't see how that's much of a problem for you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In fact I would say you are a VERY LUCKY man indeed. Frankly, I'd have fired you by now if you worked for me.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oooooh no.... You don't understand...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: This big important secret meeting that Franizzard cooked for is held here in Lumbridge castle every 10 years, and tonight will be the tenth anniversary of it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still not seeing the problem...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, I have always felt like a failure as a cook, and that my job here is down to the skills of my ancestor.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I want to prove to the world that I am a great cook too!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: But to do that... (sigh) No, it just won't work...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What won't? Come on man, pull yourself together and speak to me!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: (sniff) Weeeeelll... I thought that I would recreate the meal Franizzard cooked all of those years ago, the one that impressed

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: everybody so much... There's just this small problem though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me take a wild guess: You have the recipe but you don't have any of the ingredients, and OBVIOUSLY you can't just go and get them yourself?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yes! However did you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Call it a hunch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, tell me what you're after and then I'll decide if I want to help you or not.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, they are common enough ingredients, although I must admit the recipe I am following is very strange indeed...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I will pay you upfront for any expenses you may incur while obtaining them, and of course you may keep the change for being so helpful to me.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Please can you help me? The feast is scheduled to start very soon, and I simply cannot leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would love to help you! What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, I have an incredibly important banquet that I have to prepare for, and I am using an ancient cookbook to recreate the meal my famous ancestor made a hundred years ago.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Most of the ingredients I was able to obtain fairly easily, but there were some... oddities... that I have been unable to lay my hands on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I need the following ingredients as soon as possible!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: One Eye Of Newt A freshly made 'Dirty Blast' A rotten tomato

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: And a glass of greenman's ale.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I have given you 100 coins to cover any expenses you may incur, feel free to keep the change.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go look for those for you then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest... I just can't be bothered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh nooooo... What am I going to do now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dunno. Maybe I'll change my mind and come back later and help you.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oooohhh... I really hope you do...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know... Thinking about it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just can't be bothered to help you out. You'll have to find some other adventurer to do your little quest for you.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I guess I don't have anything more to say to you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm curious... How exactly do you still have a job?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm sorry? How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just surprising that you would still have employment as head cook of Lumbridge castle, given how obviously bad at your job you are.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: WHAT?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, things you would expect every kitchen to have... Eggs, milk, flour... You know... But you don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is worse, you don't even go off and get them yourself, you expect people like me to run around after you!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I know I'm such a failure... I bring shame to my honourable ancestor, and now I need help again...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need help AGAIN? Well, what seems to be the problem?

sections.title: Talking to the cook without all of the ingredients

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About those ingredients you wanted for the banquet...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you bring them? Remember, I need an eye of newt, a glass of greenman's ale, a rotten tomato, and a 'Dirty Blast'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... Sorry, I don't have any of that with me...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well come back when you have all of them! And hurry! It is a VERY important meeting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weeelll... I have some of that stuff with me, but not all of it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I only have [a/an] [item name] with me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I only have [a/an] [item 1] and [a/an] [item 2] with me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got a 'Dirty Blast', a Greenman's ale and a rotten tomato, I just need to get the eye of newt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the 'Dirty Blast', a Greenman's ale and the eye of newt, I just have to find myself a rotten tomato...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to find a 'Dirty Blast', a rotten tomato and an eye of newt, but I didn't manage to get a hold of a Greenman's ale yet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got you a Greenman's ale, the rotten tomato and the eye of newt, but haven't had any luck on the 'Dirty Blast' yet...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Come back and see me when you've got everything I need!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then. I'll get on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find an eye of newt?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I don't know. A newt's face?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you so much for that help.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ah, I don't know where they get them, they usually have them at herblore and magic shops.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find a rotten tomato?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm not entirely sure, but I hear they're sold for public spectacles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Public spectacles?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yeah. Pre-arranged combats, caught thieves on show, that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find a greenman's ale?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, as the official sponsored drink of Castle Wars it might be worth looking around there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find a 'Dirty Blast'? I've never heard of it before.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yes, I must admit I had never heard of it either until reading through my ancestor's cookbook.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Tell me, do you know how to make a Fruit Blast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Fruit Blast? Sure, like they sell at Blurberry's Bar, right?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ah, so you've heard of it then? But for this recipe I'd need a fresh one; you can't just buy me one from a shop or from your friends.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, a 'Dirty Blast' is a freshly made Fruit Blast, but then for some reason you add ash at the end!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Freshly made? Why did you specify that?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Apparently the ash reacts with the freshly squeezed juice in the glass. It means you can't just buy one Blurberry made earlier, you'll have to make it yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Well, it shouldn't take that long.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: That's the spirit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Fruit Blast? Can't say I've ever heard of it...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ah, well, they're a popular Gnomic delicacy served at the Blurberry bar. But for this recipe I'd need a fresh one; you can't just buy me one from a shop or from your friends.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Basically, what you do is juice a pineapple, an orange and a lemon, then put them all into a cocktail shaker.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Pour that into a glass, then top with a slice of lemon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okaaaay... So what's a 'Dirty Blast'?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, apparently after you have done the above then by adding ashes you create a unique new drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds horrible..

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I agree, but it's what the recipe specifically needs.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Bear in mind it will only work with very fresh ingredients, so you will have to make your own cocktail, not rely on one of the pre made ones Blurberry sells.

sections.title: Using ashes on a fruit blast

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That looks disgusting, but it's what the cook asked for...

sections.title: Attempting to drink the dirty blast

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, that looks DISGUSTING! I won't drink that!

sections.title: Talking to the cook with all of the ingredients

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About those ingredients you wanted for the banquet...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you bring them? Remember, I need an eye of newt, a glass of greenman's ale, a rotten tomato, and a 'Dirty Blast'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You will be happy to know... I have all of that with me!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You absolutely MUST go through into the dining room just there and see the feast I have prepared.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I simply won't take no for an answer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's that banquet going?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Why haven't you gone and looked at the feast I have prepared yet? Trust me, it will be a sight the likes of which you will never see again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know... I'm just... quite busy, I guess...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: No, honestly, you MUST go in there!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: GO IN THERE!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Welcome, gentlemen, to Lumbridge Castle.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome Osman, Spymaster for the Emir of Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I thank you for your hospitality.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome Selim, acting Spymaster for the Emir of Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Selim
lines.text: I thank you for your hospitality.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome the chief guard of the White Wolf Mountain dwarves.

lines.speaker: Mountain Dwarf
lines.text: The beer's good!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome Pirate Pete from Braindeath Island.

lines.speaker: Pirate Pete
lines.text: Your rum's got no flavour!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome the chief of the Goblin Village.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That me! Give me chair!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: No, it me! Chair mine!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome Skrach Uglogwee of the Feldip Hills ogres.

lines.speaker: Skrach Uglogwee
lines.text: Der ogres, dey call me Bone Cruncher.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I welcome my neighbour, Phileas the Lumbridge Guide.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I didn't have far to travel!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: From the town of Edgeville, I welcome Evil Dave.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: These secret meetings are SOOO EVIL!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: A hearty welcome to Sir Amik Varze, leader of the White Knights...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Do get a move on!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: ...and finally, I welcome the ruler of Ape Atoll, Awowogei.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Awowogei?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Please sit down!

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Ikik...

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: SIT DOWN!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I think he's lost his Amulet of Manspeak.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Aris seems to be late...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, Horacio!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Oh, what now?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Old Man
lines.text: Here you go Horacio, it's all yours!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Excuse me while I deal with this...

lines.speaker: Osman/Selim
lines.text: We should do something about that old man.

lines.speaker: Mountain Dwarf
lines.text: Agreed.

lines.speaker: Evil Bob
lines.text: Meoooooow!

lines.speaker: Osman/Selim
lines.text: Nooooooooooooo!

lines.speaker: Mountain Dwarf
lines.text: Wow – another one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooh – nice food!

lines.speaker: Skrach Uglogwee
lines.text: You come here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Skrach Uglogwee
lines.text: Is you my dinner?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Let go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not!

lines.speaker: Culinaromancer
lines.text: Aha! I'm BACK!

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Ek uhuh!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Did we invite you?

lines.speaker: Culinaromancer
lines.text: Hah! Your chef did! And now I'll kill you!

lines.speaker: Culinaromancer
lines.text: MUAHAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Sorry I'm late...

lines.speaker: Culinaromancer
lines.text: I remember you...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Culinaromancer
lines.text: Muahahaha!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: TEMPUS CESSIT!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: [Player name], you must help me defeat this evil!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Hello there adventurer. How can I assist you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I am the fortune-teller. My friends call me Aris.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then Aris...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Was there something you wanted to ask me about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do have a question. A very GOOD question.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Oh yes? What's that then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just what in the flipping hippo is going on?!?!?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: ...flipping hippo?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeeeess.... You've never heard that phrase?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: No, no I'm quite sure that I never have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's not the point!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, tell me what just happened!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Certainly. That fellow over there in the chef's hat is called the Culinaromancer.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: A hundred years ago, he threatened the secret council with death, but was foiled by the quick thinking of his assistant, who managed to trap him in another dimension.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: As a result of that action, the assistant was offered a job for life as head cook of Lumbridge, for himself and all his future descendants.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Now, apparently the current head cook of Lumbridge decided to recreate his ancestors greatest achievement, not realising that his achievement was not a meal but a powerful food-magic, and by recreating the spell he has

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: inadvertently freed the evil Culinaromancer from his prison, to wreak havoc upon this world once more.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Naturally, when I saw what was happening, I intervened with a simple Tempus Cessit spell, so that we would have time to immunise the secret council from his attack, and so that we can defeat the Culinaromancer

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: once and for all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... what?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: You want me to explain it again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please. But slower. And more understandable.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: (sigh) Okay then: 100 years ago, the evil food magician was defeated and banished.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Because of you and the Lumbridge cook, he is now free.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: He was about to slaughter all of these people, but I stopped time so that you could protect them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You stopped time? And... wait, I have to protect them? Why me?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes I stopped time. And YOU have to do it partly because you helped release him by getting those ingredients, and partly because I am stuck here keeping the spell intact.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't stopping time kind of... You know, dangerous?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Oh, unbelievably so. This is why I have limited the time bubble to this room and this room alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? How does that work?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Everything outside this room continues to flow through time. This is why outside of this room you have already defeated the Culinaromancer, and protected the

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: members of the secret council.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Inside this room however you have not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... what?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Where time has been frozen in this room, outside it continues to flow, so this room is now currently in what we would call 'the past', meaning events inside here have already happened, and the longer time continues to flow

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: outside, the more removed from normal time this room becomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... so I've already saved all of these council members and defeated the Culinaromancer?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes. But only outside. What you need to do now is save them in here as well, so that time can resume its normal ebb and flow.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Otherwise there will be increasing temporal pressure, and the entire universe might explode!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not the whole universe! That's where I keep my stuff!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes. That is why it is so important that you protect them all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... You said I already had...?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes. You have. In the future, which is also the present, but not in the present, which is also the past.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My brain hurts...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes, time travel tends to do that to people.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I suggest you don't worry about it too much, just make sure you protect each council member and then defeat the Culinaromancer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I do that?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Each council member will have their own unique dish that will serve to counteract the Culinaromancer's spell.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Inspect the one you wish to save, and I will offer any advice I have.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Was there anything else you wanted to ask me about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's that guy over there? Seemed like you knew each other.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: He calls himself the Culinaromancer. He is an extremely evil man, with incredibly powerful magics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Culinaromancer?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes, that's correct. Just as an Oneiromancer draws their magical powers from dreams, or a necromancer draws their strength from the energies of the dead, a Culinaromancer uses

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: food as his source of magical power.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Food? What's so dangerous about food?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Do not underestimate the latent power of foodstuffs as a source of magical energy.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Not only are they a fundamental life-force, but they often evoke great emotional attachment.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Do not underestimate him, part of his strength lies in the fact his unique type of magical power is quite obscure, I have seen many fall before him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so he's powerful, but I don't get why he's here!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ah, it is a very old story indeed.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: A hundred years ago, he worked here in Lumbridge castle for the then Duke as a cook.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: This was all a pretence, of course. As part of his duties he was allowed access to many exotic and esoteric foodstuffs. These allowed him to experiment with his magic and increase his power exponentially.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Building his power was just the first step however.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Knowing of the regular meetings of the secret council here in Lumbridge, the Culinaromancer decided that he would eliminate all of them as the first step to his plans for world domination.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would that have worked?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Possibly, we might never know. As his plans were coming to fruition his assistant discovered them, and used his own magic against him, to trap him in another dimension where his powers would

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: be useless.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how did he escape?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Well, the current cook of Lumbridge obviously discovered an ancient manuscript of his ancestors, and believing it to be a recipe decided to recreate the spell that banished him.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Which obviously seems to have opened the portal enough for him to escape back to this dimension. So this is partly your fault.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Those ingredients?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes, those ingredients. So I hold you responsible for assisting me in ridding him of this dimension once and for all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... I had a feeling you were going to say that...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Are you clear on what you have to do, or was there something you wished me to explain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are these people? Seems like quite the random selection in here!

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: The people you see assembled here are the self-styled secret council of Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Self-styled' council? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Many centuries ago, a small group from around the world of Gielinor decided that these lands needed to be directed, that it was too risky to leave the fates of this land to luck and the gods.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: They decided that every 10 years they would meet to discuss current events, and shape how the world might then develop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they're pretty influential people?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: No, not really. For all of their grand schemes and ideas, nothing ever gets done except every ten years they have a bit of a posh meal together and a bit of a gossip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... But this evil cook guy that just appeared thinks they might be important?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I don't really know what he thinks.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Either way I'm not prepared to let him get away with a brazen attack on anybody while I have the power to stop him.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: And given that this is partly your fault, I fully expect your assistance in saving them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: MY fault??

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes. You were the one who provided the cook with the ingredients that he needed to allow the Culinaromancer to return to this dimension.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just don't understand any of this...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: What exactly is it that you don't understand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... Is it me, or did this room just get much bigger?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ah, that would be the time dilation effect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The... time dilation... effect?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes. As time has stopped flowing in the normal way, your eyes are playing tricks on you, as the pictures absorbed by your eyes take slightly longer to reach your brain

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: than normal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... what?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: As everybody knows, time flows from north to south, so my freezing time means that this room has apparently stretched slightly along its horizontal axis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... does that even make sense?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Don't let it worry you too much. It's just an optical illusion, the room is exactly the same size as it always has been, it just appears to be a tiny bit bigger.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Anything else you wanted to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, forget it, I don't want to talk to you now.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure you have far more important things to do than SAVING THE ENTIRE UNIVERSE!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fancy seeing you here again.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Don't talk to me! It might cause a time paradox!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? But how can it when you're...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: LA-LA-LA I'M NOT TALKING TO YOU LA-LA-LA TIME PARADOX LA-LA-LA

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... Okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we talk about what happened in the dining room?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Wow! You were incredible! The way you defeated the Culinaromancer, you made it seem so easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... What?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: With the foods, and that big portal thingy, and everything!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm just glad you were around, who knows WHAT could have happened if you weren't able to help us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't have any clue what you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Oh, right. This must be part of that whole time-bubble thing that Aris told me about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the cook outside of the bubble remembers me having already saved the council members even though I haven't actually gone and done it yet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gah! This time travel stuff makes my head hurt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I don't suppose you can remember exactly how I defeated him can you?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Of course! Which bit can't you remember doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That dwarf... I somehow can't remember how I managed to save him...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That dwarf... I somehow can't remember how I managed to save him...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: The dwarf? Oh, yeah, I think that was an easy one, didn't you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did? Er, I did.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You went to see his dad in the tunnels under white wolf mountain, and then got some rock cakes, if I remember rightly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those bickering goblins... How did I protect them again?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect them first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, that was a weird one alright, you told me that they couldn't agree on what food they liked, so you ended up just mushing a bunch of stuff together somehow which seemed to do the trick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't quite remember how I managed to save that pirate...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't quite remember how I managed to save that pirate...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: The pirate? Yes, you used fishcakes didn't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did? Er... I mean I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Out of interest, could you just help me quickly with that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me how to make fishcakes?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Fishcakes? As in with fish eggs and fish milk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't be silly, that would never work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you check to see if there is a recipe for them?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Found it!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Apparently you need Ground Cod, Ground Kelp, Ground Giant Crab Meat and Breadcrumbs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get Ground Cod?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, if you use a pestle and mortar on a raw cod that will grind it up nicely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get Ground kelp?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: There are apparently some kelp patches off the coast near Rimmington. Murphy knows more about that.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: As for the grinding part, all you have to do is use a pestle and mortar on it, that ought to get it sorted.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Murphy also used to bring in Giant Crabs occasionally. He hasn't had any of them in for a while though.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You had best go and have a word with him to see if he can tell you what happened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get Ground Giant Crab meat?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, Murphy used to bring in Giant Crabs occasionally. He hasn't had any of them in for a while though.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: You had best go and have a word with him to see if he can tell you what happened.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh, and there are apparently some kelp patches off the coast near Rimmington. Murphy knows about that too.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: As for the grinding part, all you have to do is use a pestle and mortar on it, that ought to get it sorted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get Breadcrumbs?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: That's easy, all you do is use a knife to slice the bread up into tiny, tiny chunks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did I do with all of it?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm not entirely sure. What am I, an encyclopedia?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'll have a look through my cookbook while I wait for the ingredients.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll go out and get all of the stuff and bring it to you.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Is there anything else you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: No problem!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Lumbridge guide... I can't really rememember what I did to save him...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Lumbridge guide... I can't really rememember what I did to save him...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh, you told me that that crazy wizard in the wizards' tower helped you with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That crazy wizard at the wizards' tower? Was I not any more specific than that?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: No, not really. I think you might have mentioned the name Traiborn though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That fellow with the unhealthy obsession with black... Dave his name is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't quite remember what I did to protect him...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That fellow with the unhealthy obsession with black... Dave his name is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't quite remember what I did to protect him...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh yeah! I didn't understand that one myself, but apparently you went and asked him how he likes his stew, then made him remember it after he'd tasted it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... what?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yeah, I didn't understand it either, but you mentioned something about demonic incursions and evil cats too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just remind me again how I managed to unfreeze Skrach?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just remind me again how I managed to unfreeze Skrach?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Certainly. Everyone knows that Skrach loves Jubbly Chompy, preferably roasted on a spit! The bird's carcass is so big, it can't be cooked any other way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do I get one of those?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I wouldn't know much about that, but I believe you talked to Rantz quite a bit over in the Feldip hills. Apparently, he got you to do him quite a few favours before you actually got the Jubbly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That white knight... Sir Amik Varze... I can't quite remember how I saved him...

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: but it was something to do with creme brulee supreme...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ah yes! Why, I was so impressed with that recipe you created that I memorised it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, you did, did you? I don't suppose you could refresh my memory about that?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: But of course! It was an unusual dish, an unusual dish indeed! First you blend together, in a pot, some milk and some cream. Then add cornflour to the mixture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cornflour? How do I... er, how DID I make that then?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh, you told me you used sweetcorn in a mill!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Weird. Okay, what did I do then?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Then you travelled to the Kharazi Jungle and searched the plants there until you found a vanilla stick, which was then added to the mixture.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: After that you told me that you sought out and killed the fearsome Evil Chicken, to get one of his eggs, which was then mixed with the other ingredients.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Finally you made some cinnamon by grinding the branch of a Dramen Tree and sprinkled it on top of the creme brulee.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds easy enough so far. Er, I mean it was easy enough up to that point.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yes, but then came the impressive part!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Apparently, a creme brulee supreme needs to be flambed at exactly the right temperature, and the only fire that is hot enough to do this is the breath of a dragon. Luckily, while killing black dragons near the Evil

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Chicken's lair, you came across a method of forcing a dragon to your will so that it would complete the dish, which you then used to protect Sir Amik!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I definitely said that I had to kill some black dragons to do that?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh yes. You were quite adamant about it. Specifically black dragons. Specifically near the Evil Chicken's lair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The monkey king... However did I save him from the culinaromancer...?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Ask me how to protect him first, otherwise you will cause a time paradox! I'm keeping an eye on you, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The monkey king... However did I save him from the culinaromancer...?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, it was something that sounded ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTING that you picked up on Ape Atoll...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh MAN! That was AWESOME!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: After you managed to save all of the members of the secret council with those weird foods, the culinaromancer tried to make a run for it, but you followed him into his magic portal and Biff! Bang! Boom!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: sorted him out once and for all!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you ever know that you're my hero?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?
